title: Controlling am spatter and conduction
abstract: Presented is an intelligent feed forward model to control additive manufacturing (AM) laser powder bed fusion process and reduce spattering whereby defects are eliminated by controlling the laser power and reducing spattering through a computer model. This writing describes using a proportional integral derivative (PID) controller to create a power map that reduces spattering.
claims:
Claims THE CLAIMS ARE: Claim 1. A laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering, comprising the steps of: creating a power map to control a laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system for producing the product and reducing spattering and using said power map to control the laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system for producing the product and reducing spattering. Claim 2. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claim 1 wherein said step of using said power map to control the laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system for producing the product and reducing spattering includes the step of starting said laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system at a low power that will partially melt powder and after an interval controlling said laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system at full melt power. Claim 3. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claim 1 wherein said step of using said power map to control the laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system for producing the product and reducing spattering includes the step of first focusing said laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system at a low power that will partially melt powder and 

 second focusing said laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system at a full power that wall fully melt powder. Claim 4. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claim 1 wherein said step of using said power map to control the laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system for producing the product and reducing spattering includes the step of focusing a first laser in said laser pow der bed fusion additive manufacturing system at a low power that will partially melt powder and second focusing a second laser in said laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system at full power that will fully melt powder. Claim 5. Tire laser pownier bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claim 1 wherein said step of creating a power map includes the step of creating a tracer point in a computer model. Claim 6. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claim 1 wherein said step of using said power map to control the laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing for producing the product and reducing spattering includes the step of using a proportional integral derivative controller to produce the product. Claim 7. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claim 1 wherein said step of using said po ver map to control the laser poivder bed fusion additive 

 manufacturing for producing the product and reducing spattering includes the step of using a computational model. Claim 8. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claim 1 wherein said step of using said power map to control the laser pow ^ der bed fusion additive manufacturing for producing the product and reducing spattering includes the step of predicting laser power requirements to achieve melt pool characteristics that reduces spattering. Claim 9. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claim 1 wherein said step of using said power map to control the laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing for producing the product and reducing spattering includes the step of controlling the laser power through a computer model to predict laser power requirements to achieve melt pool characteristics that reduces spattering. Claim 10. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claim 1 wdierein said step of using said power map to control the laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing for producing the product and reducing spattering includes the step of using a proportional integral derivative controller to provide a closed control loop system to predict laser power requirements to achieve melt pool characteristics that are a function of the product geometry. Claim 11. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claim 1 wherein said step of creating a power map includes the step of using a proportional-integral- derivative loop controller for creating a power map. Claim 12. A laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering, comprising the steps of: creating a power map to control a laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system for producing the product and reducing spattering including the step of starting said laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system at a low power that will partially melt powder and after an interval controlling said laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system at full melt power, and using said power map to control the laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system for producing the product and reducing spattering. Claim 13. â lire laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claim 12 wherein said step of using said power map to control the laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system for producing the product and reducing spattering includes the step of starting said laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system at a low power that will partially melt powder and after an interval controlling said laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system at full melt power. Claim 14. A laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering using a laser beam and a powder bed containing powder particles wherein a vapor jet ejected from the powder bed entrains the powder particles in a plume, comprising the steps of: creating a power map to control a laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system for producing the product and reducing spattering including the step of determining an angle to the powder bed of said plume that reduces spattering, and using said power map to control the laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system for producing the product and reducing spattering including the step of setting the plume at said angle to the powder bed. Claim 15. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claim 14 wherein said step of creating a power map to control a laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system for producing the product and reducing spattering including the step of determining an angle to the powder bed of said plume that reduces spattering includes the step of using a proportional-integral-derivative loop controller. Claim 16. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claims 15 wherein said step of creating a power map to control a laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system for producing the product and reducing spattering including the step of determining an angle to the powder bed of said plume that 

 reduces spattering includes the step of using a camera and proportional- integral-derivative loop controller. Claim 17. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing method for producing a product and reducing spattering of claims 16 wherein said step of creating a power map to control a laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system for producing the product and reducing spattering including the step of determining an angle to the powder bed of said plume that reduces spattering includes the step of using an infrared camera and proportional-integral-derivative loop controller. Claim 18. A laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing apparatus for producing a product and reducing spattering using a laser beam and a powder bed containing powder particles wherein a vapor jet ejected from the powder bed entrains the powder particles in a plume, comprising: means for creating a power map to control a laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system, means for determining an angle to the powder bed of said plume that reduces spattering, means for using said power map to control the laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system, and means for setting the plume at said angle to the powder bed. Claim 19. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing apparatus for producing a product and reducing spattering using a laser beam and a powder bed containing powder particles wherein a vapor jet ejected from the powder bed entrains the pow er particles in a plume of claim! 8 further comprising: 

 means for using a proportional-integral-derivative loop controller for creating a power map to control a laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system and using said power map to control the laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing system. Claim 20. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing apparatus for producing a product and reducing spattering using a laser beam and a powder bed containing powder particles wherein a vapor jet ejected from the powder bed entrains the powder particles in a plume of claim 18 or 19 further comprising: means for using a camera and proportional-integral-derivative loop controller for determining an angle to the powder bed of said plume that reduces spattering and for setting the plume at said angle to the powder bed. Claim 21. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing apparatus for producing a product and reducing spattering using a laser beam and a powder bed containing powder particles wherein a vapor jet ejected from the powder bed entrains the powder particles in a plume of claim 18, or 19, further comprising: means for using an infrared camera and proportional-integral-derivative loop controller for determining an angle to the powder bed of said plume that reduces spattering and for setting the plume at said angle to the powder bed. Claim 22. The laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing apparatus for producing a product and reducing spattering using a laser beam and a powder bed containing powder particles wherein a vapor jet ejected from the powder bed entrains the powder particles in a plume of claim 20, further comprising: means for using an infrared camera and proportional -integral-derivative loop controller for determining an angle to the powder bed of said plume that reduces spattering and for setting the plume at said angle to the powder bed.
